target.title: fix: #94224 允许 cron lane 静默回复，避免空回复 FailoverError
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #94224 — Cron jobs that intentionally produce empty assistant replies fail with `FailoverError: CLI backend returned an empty response. (reason: empty_response, lane: "cron")`.

## Root Cause

The cron execution path never sets `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent`. The flag is only computed on the interactive reply path (DM/group chat). Cron lane has no path to say "an empty reply here is intentional."

## Fix

`src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts` — +2 lines

```diff
             lane: resolveCronAgentLane(params.lane),
+            allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: true,
             cliSessionId,
```

```diff
           lane: resolveCronAgentLane(params.lane),
+          allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: true,
           provider: providerOverride,
```

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Cron jobs with silent-alert pattern fail every silent tick with `FailoverError`, mark `status: error` in `cron list`. Fix passes `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: true` to both CLI and embedded agent runners.

**Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node v24.13.1, OpenClaw fix branch, DeepSeek V4 (`deepseek-chat`) as live LLM backend, Gateway on port 18790 with real cron execution.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
cd ~/workspace/openclaw
git checkout fix/issue-94224-cron-empty-reply

# Start Gateway with DeepSeek provider
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=~/.openclaw/openclaw.json node openclaw.mjs gateway --port 18790 &

# Create cron job: output only whitespace → trim() → empty
node openclaw.mjs cron add \
  --url ws://127.0.0.1:18790 --token "<token>" \
  --cron "*/3 * * * *" --session isolated --no-deliver \
  --message "Output only a single whitespace character. Nothing else."

# Wait for cron to fire, check runs
node openclaw.mjs cron runs --id <job-id>
```

**Before-fix evidence**:

`upstream/main` has zero `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` in the cron executor:
```
$ git show upstream/main:src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts | grep allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent
(zero matches)
```

Without the flag, `cli-runner.ts` throws on empty replies:
```
if (!assistantText && params.allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent !== true)
  throw new FailoverError("CLI backend returned an empty response.",
    { reason: "empty_response", lane: "cron" });
```

**After-fix evidence** (real Gateway + DeepSeek cron execution):

```
$ node openclaw.mjs cron runs --id 753509ee-...
Run: 2026-06-18T09:24:03.141+08:00
Status: ok                              ← 不是 error！
Model: deepseek/deepseek-chat
Tokens: 27088→1                         ← 模型只输出 1 token (空白字符)
Duration: 3123ms

Run: 2026-06-18T09:24:03.114+08:00
Status: ok                              ← 连续两次都 ok
Model: deepseek/deepseek-chat
Tokens: 27086→1
Duration: 3097ms
```

The model produced `output_tokens: 1` (whitespace only → `trim()` → empty string). With the fix, `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: true` skips the `FailoverError` throw and the cron status is `ok`. Without the fix, this same scenario would produce `status: error` with `FailoverError("empty_response")`.

**Observed result after the fix**: The cron executor passes `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: true` to both agent runner paths. Real cron job with near-empty LLM output (1 token of whitespace) completes with `status: ok` — the `FailoverError` is NOT thrown. Cron correctly treats empty/intentionally-minimal replies as silent success.

**What was not tested**: End-to-end with Anthropic provider (requires separate API key). The DeepSeek proof validates the full pipeline: Gateway cron scheduler → isolated agent run → LLM call → empty reply detection → flag check → `status: ok`.

## Tests and validation

```
Test Files  27 passed (27)
Tests       375 passed (375)
```

## Linked Issue

Closes #94224

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Risk Level

low

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
